evidence.scientific_clarity: Two-panel ROC+PRC with diagonal and prevalence baselines clearly conveys classifier ranking; AUROC and AP annotated in legend per brief requirements.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or rcParams set; PDF embeds DejaVu Sans as Type3 (bitmap-in-vector) rather than Type1/TrueType selectable glyphs; default DejaVu Sans font used.
evidence.palette: Four-color palette uses blue/pink/gray/green (Paul Tol-adjacent), all colorblind-distinguishable; green reserved for OursModel (directional/best), not used for generic up/down signaling.
evidence.layout_density: figsize 9x4.2 with tight margins; axes fill the space well; xlim/ylim set tightly (-0.02 to 1.02/1.05); tick spacing 0.2; no overlapping labels observed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and includes numerical claims; legend is in lower-right/lower-left avoiding curve mass; minor issue: no explicit figure label (Fig. X) in caption, and PRC legend placement (lower left) may overlap low-recall curves.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses Type3 fonts (DejaVu Sans embedded as bitmap outlines) instead of selectable Type1/TrueType; script lacks pdf.fonttype=42 setting making text non-selectable in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains both panels, references the dashed reference lines, states AUROC and AP are computed from raw scores, and concludes with specific finding: OursModel achieves highest AUROC and AP.

overall_strengths: Clean two-panel layout with all required reference lines (ROC diagonal, PRC prevalence baseline) and per-method AUROC/AP annotations in legends, fully satisfying the brief.
Colorblind-safe palette with distinct line styles (solid, dashed variants) per method and well-chosen axis limits and tick spacing make curves easy to read.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=none equivalent) means the PDF embeds DejaVu Sans as Type3 bitmap outlines — text is not selectable/searchable and the default DejaVu Sans font is used rather than a publication-quality font.
No explicit rcParams override for font family; uses matplotlib's default DejaVu Sans throughout, which is non-standard for most journals and flags the figure as auto-generated.
